name: Kinsta Static Site Hosting
description: Free static site hosting from Kinsta (now Sevalla), deploying to Cloudflare's 260+ edge locations with Git-based workflows and generous free tier limits

description.value: Free static site hosting from Kinsta (now Sevalla), deploying to Cloudflare's 260+ edge locations with Git-based workflows and generous free tier limits
description.comment: Kinsta launched Static Site Hosting in October 2023 as a free add-on to its managed hosting platform. In February 2026, application, database, and static site hosting were spun off into Sevalla, a separate PaaS brand, while Kinsta refocused on WordPress hosting. Existing services, data, and configurations were preserved in the transition.

website.comment: The original Kinsta static site hosting page still exists but the service is now managed through Sevalla (sevalla.com).

parent-company.comment: Kinsta was founded in 2013 by Mark Gavalda, Anita Dunai, Peter Sziraki, and Tom Zsomborgi. It is privately held with investment from M-One Capital and McCarthy Capital. Sevalla is Kinsta's PaaS spin-off brand.

year-launched.comment: Kinsta Static Site Hosting launched publicly on October 17-18, 2023 after an extended beta period. In February 2026, the service was transitioned to the Sevalla brand.

open-source.comment: The Kinsta/Sevalla platform is proprietary. Kinsta publishes example repositories and starter templates on GitHub, but the hosting infrastructure and platform are not open source.

primary-focus.comment: Kinsta's static site hosting is specifically focused on static sites, SPAs, and pre-rendered content. Kinsta also offers separate Application Hosting (full-stack, paid) and WordPress hosting products, but the static site hosting product is purely static.

free-tier.comment: Kinsta Static Site Hosting is entirely free with usage-based overage charges. No credit card required. Includes 100 sites, 100 GB bandwidth, and 600 build minutes per month. This is a genuine production-ready free tier, not a trial.

free-tier-bandwidth.comment: 100 GB of egress bandwidth per month per account at no cost. Only outbound bandwidth is metered. After 100 GB, overage is $0.10/GB.

free-tier-build-minutes.comment: 600 build minutes per month per account. After 600 minutes, additional build time costs $0.05/minute. Build minutes are shared across all static sites in the account.

free-tier-sites.comment: Up to 100 static sites per company/account. Sites can be deleted and recreated. All 100 sites share the same bandwidth and build minute quotas.

paid-plan-starting-price.comment: There is no paid plan tier for static site hosting. The service is free with usage-based overage: $0.10/GB for bandwidth beyond 100 GB and $0.05/minute for build time beyond 600 minutes. Kinsta's WordPress hosting starts at $35/month but that is a separate product.

bandwidth-overage-cost.comment: After the free 100 GB/month, additional bandwidth is billed at $0.10/GB with no rounding — customers pay only for actual consumption. Build minute overage is $0.05/minute.

enterprise-pricing.comment: Kinsta offers custom enterprise pricing for its broader hosting platform. Enterprise plans for WordPress hosting include custom visitor limits and dedicated resources. For static site hosting specifically, there are no published enterprise tiers — it remains usage-based.

git-integration.comment: First-party integration with GitHub, GitLab, and Bitbucket. You connect your repository during site setup and select a branch for automatic deployments. No Azure DevOps or self-hosted Git support.

auto-deploy.comment: When 'Automatic deployment on commit' is enabled during setup, pushes to the selected branch automatically trigger builds and deployments.

cli-deployment.comment: Kinsta provides a REST API for programmatic deployments. You can send a POST request to manually deploy a static site from a terminal or automated script/CI/CD pipeline (e.g., CircleCI, Jenkins). There is no dedicated standalone CLI tool like Netlify CLI, but the API provides equivalent functionality.

build-system.comment: Built-in CI/CD build pipeline using Kubernetes pods as isolated build environments. You specify a build command and publish directory. Supports Node.js-based builds with configurable Node version. Auto-detects many popular frameworks and pre-fills standard build conventions.

supported-frameworks.comment: Kinsta provides quick start templates and auto-detection for Next.js, Nuxt, Gatsby, Astro, Hugo, Jekyll, SvelteKit, Eleventy, and Vite. Hugo and Jekyll are supported despite being non-Node.js generators. Any framework that generates static output can be deployed. The build system only supports Node.js for the build process.

monorepo-support.comment: An optional 'Root directory' field during setup defines the root folder of your static site, useful for non-standard folder structures or monorepo setups.

deploy-previews.comment: Pull request previews can be enabled. When a PR is created in your Git provider, Kinsta creates a Preview environment in the Deployments section with a unique URL. Preview environments can have separate environment variable settings from production.

instant-rollbacks.comment: Previous deployments can be redeployed from the deployment history. Kinsta retains previously uploaded files across deployments using content-hash-based file naming, so redeploying a previous version requires re-uploading few to no files. While efficient, this is technically a redeploy rather than an instant atomic rollback.

deploy-notifications.comment: Email notifications can be configured for failed deployments via User Settings > Notifications in MyKinsta. Slack integration is possible via the Kinsta API with custom setup, but not built-in. No native webhook or GitHub status check integration for static site deploys.

global-cdn.comment: All static site content is pushed to Cloudflare's global CDN with 260+ edge locations. Content is stored in Cloudflare R2 bucket storage and cached at edge servers worldwide.

cdn-provider.comment: Kinsta uses Cloudflare's CDN and edge network for all static site hosting. Cloudflare Enterprise integration is included. Static assets are stored in Cloudflare R2 and served from Cloudflare's edge.

edge-locations.comment: Kinsta/Sevalla documents 260+ Cloudflare edge locations across 100+ countries for static site hosting. Some marketing materials reference 300+ locations, which is the broader Cloudflare network number. The 260+ figure is specifically documented for the static site hosting product.

http2.comment: HTTP/2 is supported through Cloudflare's infrastructure. All Kinsta sites served through Cloudflare benefit from HTTP/2 with multiplexed streams and header compression.

http3.comment: HTTP/3 (QUIC) is supported through Cloudflare's infrastructure. Kinsta CDN is HTTP/3-enabled and automatically falls back to HTTP/2 for browsers that don't support HTTP/3.

response-headers.comment: Kinsta Static Site Hosting does not provide a self-service way to configure custom response headers. For WordPress hosting, Kinsta support can add headers to the Nginx configuration, but this option is not documented for static sites. Cloudflare may add some security headers automatically.

image-optimization.comment: Kinsta Static Site Hosting does not offer built-in image optimization or transformation. Images are served as-is from the CDN. Kinsta does offer image optimization for WordPress hosting (via CDN), but this is a separate product.

asset-compression.comment: Static sites served through Cloudflare use Brotli compression by default, with gzip as a fallback for clients that don't support Brotli. Compression is applied automatically by Cloudflare's edge network.

custom-domains.comment: Custom domains can be added on the Domains page in MyKinsta. Sites also get a temporary kinsta.page subdomain for testing before connecting a custom domain.

free-ssl.comment: Free SSL certificates are automatically provisioned and renewed for all custom domains. Temporary kinsta.page domains are covered by the wildcard *.kinsta.page certificate.

wildcard-ssl.comment: When adding a domain, you can select the Wildcard option to cover all subdomains. The temporary kinsta.page domains use a wildcard certificate by default. Custom wildcard SSL certificates can also be used.

auto-https-redirect.comment: All content is served over SSL by default through Cloudflare. HTTP to HTTPS redirection is handled automatically by Cloudflare's infrastructure.

custom-domain-preview.comment: Preview deployments use auto-generated URLs. Custom domains cannot be assigned to preview environments; only the production deployment supports custom domains.

serverless-functions.comment: Kinsta Static Site Hosting does not include serverless functions. The platform explicitly does not support server-side code execution for static sites. Kinsta's separate Application Hosting product supports server-side applications but is a paid service.

functions-runtime.comment: Not applicable. Kinsta Static Site Hosting does not offer serverless functions.

edge-functions.comment: No edge functions are available for static site hosting. Kinsta's infrastructure uses Cloudflare Workers internally for routing, but this is not exposed to users. The platform has mentioned expanding to Function-as-a-Service at the edge in the future.

scheduled-functions.comment: Not available. No serverless function support means no scheduled function support for static sites.

form-handling.comment: No built-in form handling. Kinsta recommends third-party services like Getform or Formspree for form submissions on static sites.

authentication.comment: No built-in authentication service for static sites. Server-side authentication and sessions are explicitly listed as unsupported. Third-party auth providers must be used.

kv-storage.comment: No key-value storage available for static site hosting users. Kinsta uses Cloudflare Workers KV internally for routing, but this is not exposed as a user-facing feature.

database-integration.comment: No database integration for static sites. Database connections are explicitly listed as unsupported. Kinsta offers separate database hosting (MySQL, PostgreSQL, Redis, MariaDB) as a paid product.

redirects.comment: Kinsta Static Site Hosting does not support URL redirects (301, 302). This is a known limitation discussed in the community forums. Redirects are available for WordPress hosting but not for static sites.

rewrites.comment: URL rewrites and reverse proxying are not supported for static sites. The platform serves files directly from the CDN without rewrite rule capabilities.

custom-404.comment: An 'Error file' setting allows specifying which file to serve when a navigation error (404) occurs. This can point to a custom 404.html or to index.html for SPA routing.

spa-fallback.comment: SPA fallback routing is supported through the 'Error file' setting, which can be set to index.html to redirect all navigation errors to the SPA entry point for client-side routing.

headers-config.comment: No self-service custom headers configuration is available for static sites. This is a limitation compared to competitors like Netlify, Vercel, and Cloudflare Pages which support _headers files or configuration-based header rules.

ab-testing.comment: No A/B testing or traffic splitting features are available for static site hosting.

free-team-members.comment: No documented limit on the number of users that can be invited to a company account. Users can be granted Company-level roles (Owner, Administrator, Developer, Billing) or service-level roles (Static Site Administrator).

rbac.comment: MyKinsta supports multiple role levels: Company Owner, Company Administrator, Company Developer, Company Billing at the company level, and Static Site Administrator at the service level. Users can be assigned access to specific static sites.

audit-logs.comment: No documented audit log feature for static site hosting actions. Deployment history is available but a comprehensive audit trail of all user actions is not documented.

environment-variables.comment: Environment variables can be configured as key-value pairs for the build process. Variables can be scoped to production environment, preview environment, or both. Changes to environment variables require a site redeploy to take effect.

preview-comments.comment: No built-in visual commenting or annotation system for deploy previews. Preview deployments generate URLs for review but lack collaborative commenting features.

ddos-protection.comment: DDoS protection is provided through Cloudflare's infrastructure. All traffic is routed through Cloudflare which delivers Layer 3, 4, and 7 DDoS protection and blocks malicious connections at the network edge. Kinsta reports 200 DDoS attacks automatically mitigated in a 30-day period in 2024.

waf.comment: Web Application Firewall is provided through Cloudflare Enterprise integration at no extra cost. Kinsta reports 3.3 billion requests mitigated by Cloudflare's WAF in a 30-day period in 2024. WAF rulesets are managed and regularly updated.

soc2.comment: Kinsta achieved SOC 2 Type II compliance in August 2023, covering Security, Availability, and Confidentiality trust service criteria. The company also holds ISO 27001, ISO 27017, and ISO 27018 certifications.

gdpr.comment: Kinsta is GDPR compliant with a published Data Processing Addendum. Compliant with EU-U.S. Data Privacy Framework, UK Extension, and Swiss-U.S. Data Privacy Framework. Also CCPA compliant. Infrastructure runs on Google Cloud Platform.

password-protection.comment: Password protection (HTTP basic auth) is available for Kinsta's WordPress hosting via the htpasswd tool, but is not documented as available for static site hosting.

ip-access-control.comment: IP access control (allowlists/denylists) is available for WordPress hosting SFTP/SSH access but is not documented for static site hosting.

documentation-quality.comment: Documentation covers getting started, build configuration, deployments, domains, SSL, and environment variables. However, documentation is in flux due to the Kinsta-to-Sevalla transition, and some advanced topics (custom headers, redirects, detailed routing) are not well documented. The blog post 'Under the Hood' provides good technical depth. Community forum fills some gaps but official docs have notable omissions.

integrations.comment: Kinsta provides built-in analytics and bandwidth monitoring in the MyKinsta dashboard. A REST API is available for programmatic management. The integration ecosystem is limited compared to Netlify or Vercel — no first-party CMS, auth, database, commerce, or search integrations for static sites specifically. Kinsta's broader platform has more integrations but they don't apply to static site hosting.

community.comment: Kinsta has an active community forum (Discourse-based) with a dedicated Static Sites category. Stack Overflow has Kinsta-related questions. No official Discord or GitHub Discussions. Support is primarily through chat in MyKinsta.

status-page.comment: Public status page at status.kinsta.com covering the website, control panel, and hosting platform. Users can subscribe to status updates. Issues affecting a small number of customers are reported directly in MyKinsta.
